claim: Anthropic's official YouTube channel (@anthropic-ai) has 569K subscribers and 168 videos as of April 2026, dominated by short product demos (Opus 4.6 launch, Cowork, Claude Code, MCP) plus longer interpretability/safety pieces like 'What is AI reward hacking?' (52 min) and 'When AIs act emotional', posting roughly weekly.
quote: Anthropic @anthropic-ai • 569K subscribers • 168 videos We're an AI safety and research company. ... Introducing Claude Opus 4.6 ... Cowork: Claude Code for the rest of your work ... When AIs act emotional ... What is AI 'reward hacking'

claim: OpenAI's official YouTube channel (@OpenAI, 1.94M subscribers, 525 videos as of April 2026) is overwhelmingly product-marketing — ChatGPT use-case ads, Codex spotlights, GPT-5.x demo clips, the OpenAI Podcast, and replays of openai.com/live launch streams — rather than research talks.
quote: OpenAI @OpenAI • 1.94M subscribers • 525 videos ... OpenAI Podcast Ep. 17 ... Introducing GPT-5.5 with Perplexity ... ChatGPT Images 2.0 ... Codex for (almost) everything ... What Codex Unlocks for Ramp

claim: NeurIPS does not push paper talks to YouTube — recordings live on the conference's own virtual platform (nips.cc/virtual/<year>) about a month after the event, behind a registration wall.
quote: All recordings will be available about a month after the conference ... We do not use Whova for videos.

claim: High-profile NeurIPS talks like Ilya Sutskever's 2024 Test-of-Time keynote 'Sequence to sequence learning with neural networks: what a decade' surface on YouTube only as third-party reuploads, not from an official NeurIPS account.
quote: Ilya Sutskever: "Sequence to sequence learning with neural networks: what a decade" — full talk at NeurIPS 2024 in Vancouver, Canada

claim: OpenAI runs a near-continuous livestream cadence (DevDay 2025 keynote on Oct 6 plus standalone product streams Feb–Oct 2025 and Apr 2026), all archived to its YouTube channel as the official replay home.
quote: OpenAI hosted their third annual DevDay on October 6, 2025 at Fort Mason in San Francisco. The keynote was livestreamed on openai.com, and all other sessions were recorded and shared afterward.

claim: NeurIPS announces marquee content (Test-of-Time paper awards, oral selections) on its blog and X account rather than as YouTube videos — confirming the channel is not the primary distribution surface for the conference.
quote: Announcing the NeurIPS 2024 Test of Time Paper Awards

claim: Anthropic's product-launch beats (Claude Design in April 2026, Claude Opus 4.6 with 1M-token context in February 2026, MCP, Claude Code) are first announced on anthropic.com and then echoed as 30-second-to-2-minute demo videos on YouTube — making the channel a marketing mirror, not a primary research feed.
quote: Introducing Claude Design by Anthropic Labs

claim: Curated 2026 'best AI YouTube channels' lists targeting practitioners typically lead with educator/explainer channels (Two Minute Papers, DeepLearning.AI, 3Blue1Brown, AI Explained, Yannic Kilcher) and do not single out the OpenAI, Anthropic, or NeurIPS official channels as primary subscriptions.
quote: Some of the best AI YouTube channels include Two Minute Papers, DeepLearning.AI, AI Explained, Tina Huang, The AI Advantage, How I AI, DigitalOcean, Skill Leap AI, Wes Roth, Dwarkesh Patel
